posts._note: Talk about the problem. No link in first 3-5 lines. Make them feel the frustration. Then reveal the guide.
posts.linkedin:
You posted again today.

200 impressions. 2 likes. Silence.

You wrote something real. Something you actually care about. And LinkedIn responded with a quiet, indifferent nothing.

So you wonder: is my content bad? Is my account shadowbanned? Do I just not have enough followers yet?

None of those are the real answer.

The real answer is structure. Specifically, your first two lines.

LinkedIn's algorithm makes its distribution decision in the first 60–90 minutes after you post. It looks at one primary signal: how many people are clicking "see more" to expand your post. That rate is almost entirely determined by your hook — not your insights, not your credentials, not your follower count.

I built a free guide that breaks this down completely — the V.I.S.I.B.L.E.™ Framework, 5 proven hook types, the S.T.A.C.K. storytelling model, and a 30-day implementation calendar.

It's 10 pages. Free. No email wall.

👉 Download it here: https://publixion.com/guides/linkedin-engagement-guide

#LinkedIn #ContentMarketing #PersonalBrand #Publixion

posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
Every high-performing LinkedIn post has the same 7-layer structure.

Most creators only get 1 or 2 of these right. That's why they plateau.

Here's the V.I.S.I.B.L.E.™ Framework from our free guide:

→ V — Value Positioning: Answer "why does this matter to my reader" before you write a single word. No clear value = no foundation.

→ I — Interrupt Hook: First two lines must stop the scroll. Contrarian claim, specific outcome, or pain amplification. Generic openings die before the algorithm can act.

→ S — Structured Formatting: 1–2 sentence paragraphs. White space. Short bullets. LinkedIn is mobile-first. Design for scanning, not reading.

→ I — Insightful Story: Use the S.T.A.C.K. Model™ — Situation, Tension, Action, Change, Key Lesson. Stories generate 3× more comments than generic advice.

→ B — Behavioral Trigger: Give people a reason to comment, not just react. Comments = 4× the algorithmic weight of a like.

→ L — Leverage Algorithm: Post at consistent times. Engage before posting. Reply to every comment in the first hour. No external links in the body.

→ E — Engagement Loop: Reply fast, ask follow-ups, DM qualified commenters, repurpose your best posts.

Weaken any one of these layers and the post underperforms — regardless of your insights.

👉 Full guide free at: https://publixion.com/guides/linkedin-engagement-guide

#LinkedIn #ContentStrategy #PersonalBrand #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
Here's how a consultant used this guide to go from invisible to $2,500 in new business.

Before the guide:
→ 800 average impressions per post
→ 1–2 likes per post
→ 0 inbound messages from content

Step 1: She ran the Engagement Diagnostic from the guide. Engagement rate: 0.3%. Below the 2% threshold — the guide flagged hooks as the issue.

Step 2: She rewrote her last 3 post hooks using the 5 hook types in the framework. Repotted all 3 the following week.

Step 3: She applied the pre-post routine from the guide — 10 minutes of strategic commenting before publishing. Replied to every comment within the first hour.

Step 4: She added a lead CTA to every post: "DM me 'Audit' if you want free feedback on your content."

After 30 days:
→ 6,500 average impressions
→ 30–70 reactions per post
→ 3 inbound leads from content
→ 1 new client — $2,500 project

The content didn't change. The structure did.

👉 Free guide: https://publixion.com/guides/linkedin-engagement-guide

posts._note: Handle the top doubts: price, time, skill level, whether it will work for them.
posts.linkedin:
"The LinkedIn algorithm is too unpredictable. No system can fix it consistently."

I hear this a lot. Here's the reality:

The algorithm isn't random. It rewards four specific things:

1. Early engagement in the first 60–90 minutes
2. Meaningful comments (5+ words) over shallow reactions
3. Consistent posting at predictable times
4. High dwell time — posts people read slowly or re-read

All four are within your direct control.

"My industry is boring. No one engages with serious topics."

Wrong frame. The issue isn't the topic — it's the hook. A boring topic with a great hook outperforms an exciting topic with a weak one every time.

"I don't have time to engage before and after every post."

The system requires 10 minutes before posting and 60 minutes of availability after. That's not a time problem — it's a priority problem.

"I already tried posting consistently for months. Nothing changed."

Consistency without structure is just noise. This guide adds structure to your consistency.

Free. 10 pages. No catch.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/linkedin-engagement-guide

#LinkedIn #Objections #ContentMarketing #Publixion
